title: Diagnose poor search recall
description: Work through metric mismatch, query parameters, index training, and filtering to find why neighbours are wrong.
body: Use when an ANN search returns visibly wrong or incomplete neighbours. Actions, in order: confirm the query metric matches the index's build metric; raise ef_search or nprobe and re-measure recall; for IVFFlat, check the index was built on populated data with enough lists; and verify any metadata filter is applied as a pre-filter, not a post-filter. Compare each change against brute-force ground truth. Verify recall recovers after the correct cause is addressed. Validation signals include recall@k climbing back to target once the root cause is fixed. Risk boundary: silently low recall corrupts every downstream result, so treat it as a correctness defect and gate release on a recall threshold, not just on latency.
